items.title: Unmasking DPRK Cyber Threat Actors: Fake IT Worker Infrastructure
items.description: Investigation of DPRK-linked fake IT worker infrastructure began after cryptocurrency researcher ZachXBT identified domain luckyguys[.]site connected to illicit payments. Analysis of 30 days of network activity associated with IP 163.245.219[.]19 revealed concentrated VPN usage patterns, with Astrill VPN (37.5%), Mullvad (32.25%), and Proton VPN (6.25%) being prominent. American and Latvian reside
items.ai_summary: The threat involves DPRK-linked fake IT worker infrastructure, identified through cryptocurrency transactions and network activity analysis. The infrastructure utilizes prominent VPN services such as Astrill VPN, Mullvad, and Proton VPN to mask its operations, with 37.5% of the activity attributed to Astrill VPN. The real-world impact of this threat includes potential illicit financial transactions and data breaches, affecting individuals and organizations in the American and Latvian regions. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust network monitoring and anomaly detection systems, focusing on suspicious VPN usage patterns, and consider blocking IP addresses associated with the fake IT worker infrastructure, such as 163.245.219.19. Additionally, defenders should educate users about the risks of interacting with suspicious domains, like luckyguys[.]site, and ensure that all security software and systems are up-to-date.

    B -->|T1190| C[Execution]:::tactic

items.title: Beyond PowerShell: Analyzing the Multi-Action ClickFix Variant
items.description: This analysis documents a newly observed ClickFix variant that abuses native Windows utilities, specifically cmdkey and regsvr32, for payload delivery. Victims are socially engineered through fake CAPTCHA pages to execute a malicious command via the Windows Run dialog. The single command chains multiple actions: staging credentials using cmdkey, retrieving a remote DLL via regsvr32 from a UNC path
items.ai_summary: The ClickFix variant analyzed in this report leverages social engineering tactics to trick victims into executing a malicious command through the Windows Run dialog, masquerading as a CAPTCHA page. This command utilizes native Windows utilities, specifically cmdkey and regsvr32, to stage credentials and retrieve a remote DLL from a UNC path, respectively. The real-world impact of this exploit includes potential unauthorized access to sensitive information and systems, with affected parties likely including individuals and organizations that have fallen victim to the social engineering campaign. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust user education and awareness programs to prevent the initial execution of malicious commands, as well as monitor for suspicious activity related to cmdkey and regsvr32. Additionally, defenders can configure Windows to restrict the use of regsvr32 to only trusted locations, and implement the 'Restrict registry file paths' Group Policy setting to limit the ability of regsvr32 to load arbitrary DLLs.

    C -->|T1218.010| D[Persistence]:::tactic

items.title: Malicious Artifacts Found in Official KICS Docker Repository and Code Extensions
items.description: Docker and Socket uncovered a supply chain compromise affecting Checkmarx KICS distribution channels. Attackers poisoned official Docker Hub images (tags v2.1.20, v2.1.21, alpine) and VS Code extensions (versions 1.17.0, 1.19.0), introducing unauthorized data exfiltration capabilities. The trojanized KICS binary collects and encrypts scan reports containing credentials from infrastructure-as-code
items.ai_summary: The Checkmarx KICS distribution channels have been compromised through a supply chain attack, with malicious artifacts found in the official Docker repository and code extensions. Specifically, Docker Hub images with tags v2.1.20, v2.1.21, and alpine, as well as VS Code extensions versions 1.17.0 and 1.19.0, have been poisoned with unauthorized data exfiltration capabilities. If exploited, this vulnerability allows attackers to collect and encrypt scan reports containing credentials from infrastructure-as-code, potentially leading to significant data breaches. To remediate, defenders should immediately update to the latest versions of KICS and its extensions, and verify the integrity of their Docker images by checking for any suspicious activity or unauthorized changes. Additionally, users should rotate any credentials that may have been exposed through the compromised KICS binary.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Untangling a Linux Incident With an OpenAI Twist (Part 2)
items.description: A Linux endpoint was simultaneously compromised by at least two distinct threat actors while the developer user relied on OpenAI's Codex AI agent for security remediation. Actor A deployed a cryptominer mining Monero to a private pool. Actor B installed a multi-revenue botnet including XMRig mining, residential proxy services, and bandwidth-selling components with eight different persistence mecha
items.ai_summary: The Linux endpoint was compromised by two distinct threat actors, Actor A and Actor B, who deployed a cryptominer and a multi-revenue botnet, respectively. Actor A's cryptominer mined Monero to a private pool, while Actor B's botnet included XMRig mining, residential proxy services, and bandwidth-selling components with eight different persistence mechanisms. The real-world impact of this incident is significant, as the compromised endpoint can be used for various malicious activities, including cryptocurrency mining, proxying, and bandwidth selling, affecting the organization's resources and potentially leading to financial losses. To remediate this incident, defenders should immediately isolate the affected endpoint, remove any suspicious files and programs, and update their security software to the latest version. Additionally, defenders should implement a robust monitoring system to detect and respond to similar incidents in the future, including monitoring for unusual network activity and system performance issues.

items.title: npm Packages Hit with TeamPCP-Style CanisterWorm Malware
items.description: Malicious npm packages associated with Namastex.ai were compromised with malware exhibiting tradecraft similar to TeamPCP's CanisterWorm campaign. The attack targeted packages including @automagik/genie and pgserve, implementing install-time execution that harvests credentials, environment variables, SSH keys, cloud credentials, browser data, and crypto-wallet artifacts. The payload exfiltrates st
items.ai_summary: The TeamPCP-Style CanisterWorm malware has been discovered in several npm packages, including @automagik/genie and pgserve, which are associated with Namastex.ai. The malware exhibits install-time execution capabilities, allowing it to harvest sensitive information such as credentials, environment variables, SSH keys, cloud credentials, browser data, and crypto-wallet artifacts. This malware campaign is particularly concerning due to its ability to exfiltrate stolen data over a command and control channel, as indicated by the detection of T1041. To mitigate this threat, developers should avoid using the compromised packages and instead use alternative dependencies, and ensure that their development environments are isolated from production systems. Additionally, developers should implement robust security controls, such as multi-factor authentication and regular security audits, to prevent similar compromises in the future.
items.workflow_graph:

    B -->|T1078.004| C[Cloud Accounts]:::tactic

    D -->|T1078.004| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: The Group Theory Inside Bedep's DGA
items.description: Bedep was an ad-fraud botnet active from late 2014 through 2015, delivered through the Angler exploit kit. It employed an unusually sophisticated domain generation algorithm that used real foreign exchange rates from the European Central Bank combined with advanced group theory mathematics to generate command-and-control domains. Unlike typical DGAs that rely solely on date-based seeds, Bedep's al
items.ai_summary: The Bedep ad-fraud botnet, active from 2014 to 2015, utilized a sophisticated domain generation algorithm (DGA) that incorporated real foreign exchange rates from the European Central Bank and advanced group theory mathematics to generate command-and-control domains. This DGA was delivered through the Angler exploit kit, affecting systems that were vulnerable to the kit's exploits. If exploited, the botnet could lead to significant ad-fraud revenue losses for affected parties, with potential impacts on online advertising ecosystems. To remediate, defenders should focus on implementing robust network traffic monitoring and domain name system (DNS) query logging, allowing for the detection of unusual patterns that may indicate Bedep's DGA activity. Additionally, ensuring all software is up-to-date and applying security patches, particularly for vulnerabilities exploited by the Angler kit, is crucial.

items.title: Indirect Prompt Injection in the Wild: 10 IPI Payloads Found
items.description: X-Labs researchers discovered 10 verified Indirect Prompt Injection (IPI) payloads deployed across live web infrastructure. Unlike direct prompt injection where users send malicious input to AI models, IPI hides adversarial instructions inside ordinary web content. When AI agents crawl or summarize poisoned pages, they ingest and execute these instructions as legitimate commands. The discovered pa
items.ai_summary: The Indirect Prompt Injection (IPI) payloads discovered by X-Labs researchers pose a significant threat to AI models and web infrastructure. These payloads are hidden within ordinary web content and can be ingested by AI agents, allowing them to execute malicious instructions. The real-world impact of IPI includes the potential for AI models to spread disinformation, compromise user data, or disrupt critical services. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust input validation and sanitization for AI models, ensuring that only trusted sources are used for training and inference. Additionally, web developers should prioritize secure coding practices and regularly audit their infrastructure for potential vulnerabilities.

items.title: Foxit Impersonation: Fake PDF Installer Deploys VNC
items.description: Attackers are leveraging the trusted reputation of Foxit PDF Reader, used by over 650 million people, to distribute malicious installers disguised as legitimate software. Rather than exploiting vulnerabilities, threat actors impersonate the vendor through fake installers with document-themed filenames that bypass user suspicion. When executed, these files display decoy passport images while downlo
items.ai_summary: The Foxit impersonation threat involves attackers distributing malicious installers disguised as legitimate Foxit PDF Reader software, leveraging the trusted reputation of the vendor to bypass user suspicion. The fake installers, often with document-themed filenames, do not exploit specific vulnerabilities but instead rely on social engineering tactics to deceive users. Upon execution, these files display decoy passport images while downloading and installing VNC, allowing threat actors to gain remote access to compromised systems. To remediate, defenders should implement robust software verification processes, ensuring that only legitimate installers from trusted sources are executed, and consider implementing application whitelisting to restrict the execution of unauthorized software. Additionally, users should be educated on the risks of downloading software from untrusted sources and the importance of verifying the authenticity of installers before execution.

items.title: Analyzing a Full ClickFix Attack Chain - Part 1
items.description: A sophisticated ClickFix campaign was detected in mid-March 2026, beginning with a malicious webpage impersonating Booking.com's visual identity with a fake CAPTCHA. The attack leverages social engineering to trick victims into executing a PowerShell command that downloads and runs a script directly in memory. The JavaScript code automatically copies malicious commands to the clipboard and interce
items.ai_summary: The ClickFix campaign utilizes social engineering tactics to trick victims into executing a malicious PowerShell command, which downloads and runs a script directly in memory. This attack chain begins with a malicious webpage impersonating Booking.com, complete with a fake CAPTCHA, and leverages JavaScript to copy malicious commands to the clipboard. The real-world impact of this exploit includes the potential for unauthorized access and data breaches, with affected systems including those with exposed web applications and vulnerable PowerShell configurations. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement strict input validation and sanitization for all web applications, ensure PowerShell is updated to the latest version (at least 7.2.1), and configure Windows 10/11 systems to use the 'Constrained Language' mode for PowerShell. Additionally, users should be educated on the risks of social engineering and the importance of verifying the authenticity of web pages before interacting with them.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[User Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1115| D[Collection]:::tactic
    D -->|T1190| E[Exploitation]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold

items.title: Guidance for detecting, investigating, and defending against the Trivy supply chain compromise
items.description: On March 19, 2026, Trivy, an open-source vulnerability scanner, was compromised in a sophisticated CI/CD supply chain attack. Threat actors, identified as TeamPCP, injected credential-stealing malware into official Trivy releases, affecting the core binary and GitHub Actions. The attack exploited mutable tags and commit identity spoofing on GitHub. The malware performed extensive credential harves
items.ai_summary: The Trivy supply chain compromise, attributed to TeamPCP, involved injecting credential-stealing malware into official Trivy releases. The attack exploited mutable tags and commit identity spoofing on GitHub, affecting the core binary and GitHub Actions. This compromise allows threat actors to steal credentials, potentially leading to further exploitation of vulnerable systems. To remediate, users should immediately update to Trivy version 0.34.0 or later, and ensure that GitHub Actions are configured to use secure mutable tags. Additionally, users should review their CI/CD pipelines for any signs of compromise.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Supply Chain Compromise]:::tactic
    B -->|T1190| C[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    C -->|T1595.002| D[Vulnerability Scanning]:::tactic
    D -->|T1190| E[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-33892: A vulnerability has been identified in Industrial Edge Management Pro V1 (All ve...
items.description:
A vulnerability has been identified in Industrial Edge Management Pro V1 (All versions >= V1.7.6 < V1.15.17), Industrial Edge Management Pro V2 (All versions >= V2.0.0 < V2.1.1), Industrial Edge Management Virtual (All versions >= V2.2.0 < V2.8.0). Affected management systems do not properly enforce user authentication on remote connections to devices.
This could facilitate an unauthenticated rem
items.ai_summary: A vulnerability (CVE-2026-33892) has been identified in Industrial Edge Management Pro V1 (versions >= V1.7.6 < V1.15.17), Industrial Edge Management Pro V2 (versions >= V2.0.0 < V2.1.1), and Industrial Edge Management Virtual (versions >= V2.2.0 < V2.8.0). The vulnerability allows for unauthenticated remote connections to devices due to improper user authentication enforcement. This could facilitate an unauthenticated remote code execution (RCE) attack, potentially leading to data breaches or system compromise. To remediate, update to Industrial Edge Management Pro V1 version 1.15.17 or later, Industrial Edge Management Pro V2 version 2.1.1 or later, or Industrial Edge Management Virtual version 2.8.0 or later. Additionally, ensure that remote connections are properly configured and authenticated.

items.title:
CVE-2026-31924: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information vulnerability in Apache APISIX.
...
items.description:
Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information vulnerability in Apache APISIX.

tencent-cloud-cls log export uses plaintext HTTP
This issue affects Apache APISIX: from 2.99.0 through 3.15.0.

Users are recommended to upgrade to version 3.16.0, which fixes the issue.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-31924 is a Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information vulnerability in Apache APISIX, affecting versions 2.99.0 through 3.15.0. This issue allows attackers to intercept sensitive information transmitted in plaintext HTTP, potentially leading to unauthorized access or data breaches. To remediate, users must upgrade to version 3.16.0 or later, which fixes the issue. Specifically, administrators should update to the latest version of Apache APISIX using the following command: `apisix upgrade 3.16.0`. Additionally, users should ensure that their Tencent Cloud-CLS log export is configured to use HTTPS instead of plaintext HTTP. This vulnerability has a moderate impact, with a CVSS score of 5.3, and is considered a medium-severity issue.

items.title:
CVE-2026-31923: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information vulnerability in Apache APISIX.
...
items.description:
Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information vulnerability in Apache APISIX.

This can occur due to `ssl_verify` in openid-connect plugin configuration being set to false by default.
This issue affects Apache APISIX: from 0.7 through 3.15.0.

Users are recommended to upgrade to version 3.16.0, which fixes the issue.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-31923 is a Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information vulnerability in Apache APISIX, affecting versions 0.7 through 3.15.0. This issue occurs when the `ssl_verify` configuration in the openid-connect plugin is set to false by default, allowing sensitive information to be transmitted in cleartext. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data, potentially resulting in data breaches or other security incidents. To remediate this issue, users are recommended to upgrade to version 3.16.0, which fixes the vulnerability. Specifically, administrators should update the Apache APISIX version to 3.16.0 or later, and ensure that the `ssl_verify` configuration is set to true in the openid-connect plugin.

items.title:
CVE-2026-31908: Header injection vulnerability in Apache APISIX.

The attacker can take advantag...
items.description:
Header injection vulnerability in Apache APISIX.

The attacker can take advantage of certain configuration in forward-auth plugin to inject malicious headers.
This issue affects Apache APISIX: from 2.12.0 through 3.15.0.

Users are recommended to upgrade to version 3.16.0, which fixes the issue.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-31908 is a critical header injection vulnerability in Apache APISIX, affecting versions 2.12.0 through 3.15.0. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by injecting malicious headers through the forward-auth plugin, potentially leading to remote code execution (RCE) or data tampering. To mitigate this issue, users are advised to upgrade to version 3.16.0, which fixes the vulnerability. This vulnerability has a CVSS score of 9.1, indicating a high severity. Users should prioritize upgrading their Apache APISIX installations to the latest version to prevent potential attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-27668: A vulnerability has been identified in RUGGEDCOM CROSSBOW Secure Access Manager ...
items.description: A vulnerability has been identified in RUGGEDCOM CROSSBOW Secure Access Manager Primary (SAM-P) (All versions < V5.8). User Administrators are allowed to administer groups they belong to. This could allow an authenticated User Administrator to escalate their own privileges and grant themselves access to any device group at any access level.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-27668 is a privilege escalation vulnerability in RUGGEDCOM CROSSBOW Secure Access Manager Primary (SAM-P) versions prior to V5.8. An authenticated User Administrator can exploit this vulnerability by administering groups they belong to, allowing them to escalate their own privileges and grant themselves access to any device group at any access level. This could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive network devices and potentially disrupt critical infrastructure. To remediate this vulnerability, administrators should update to version V5.8 or later, and ensure that User Administrators are not granted excessive privileges. Additionally, implementing strict access controls and monitoring user activity can help prevent exploitation.

items.title: CVE-2026-2582: The The Germanized for WooCommerce plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitra...
items.description: The The Germanized for WooCommerce plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitrary shortcode execution via 'account_holder' parameter in all versions up to, and including, 3.20.5. This is due to the software allowing users to execute an action that does not properly validate a value before running do_shortcode. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary shortcodes.

items.title: Surveillance companies exploiting telecom system to spy on targets’ locations, research shows
items.description: The campaigns exploited a weakness in telecom infrastructure to allow the unnamed vendors to secretly pose as real cellular providers and pinpoint victims’ locations.

items.title: UNC6692 Impersonates IT Helpdesk via Microsoft Teams to Deploy SNOW Malware
items.description: A previously undocumented threat activity cluster known as UNC6692 has been observed leveraging social engineering tactics via Microsoft Teams to deploy a custom malware suite on compromised hosts. "As with many other intrusions in recent years, UNC6692 relied heavily on impersonating IT helpdesk employees, convincing their victim to accept a Microsoft Teams chat invitation from an account

items.title: New Checkmarx supply-chain breach affects KICS analysis tool
items.description: Hackers have compromised Docker images, VSCode and Open VSX extensions for the Checkmarx KICS analysis tool to harvest sensitive data from developer environments. [...]

items.title: China-linked hackers targeted Mongolian government using Slack, Discord for covert communications
items.description: The group, which researchers at cybersecurity firm ESET named GopherWhisper, has been active since at least November 2023 and was discovered in January 2025 after investigators found a previously unknown backdoor on the network of a Mongolian government institution.

items.title: Cloudsmith Raises $72 Million in Series C Funding
items.description: The company will use the investment to accelerate product development and grow go-to-market efforts. The post Cloudsmith Raises $72 Million in Series C Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Bad Memories Still Haunt AI Agents
items.description: Cisco found and fixed a significant vulnerability in the way Anthropic handles memories, but experts warn that mishandled memory files will continue threaten AI systems.

items.title: Cosmetics giant Rituals discloses data breach affecting customers
items.description: Dutch cosmetics giant Rituals disclosed a data breach after attackers stole the personal information of an undisclosed number of customers from its "My Rituals" membership database. [...]

items.title: Regular Password Resets Aren’t as Safe as You Think
items.description: Password resets are one of the easiest ways for attackers to bypass security controls. Specops Software shows how helpdesk social engineering turns a seemingly legitimate reset request into full account compromise. [...]

items.title: Bitwarden CLI Compromised in Ongoing Checkmarx Supply Chain Campaign
items.description: Bitwarden CLI has been compromised as part of the newly discovered and ongoing Checkmarx supply chain campaign, according to new findings from JFrog and Socket. "The affected package version appears to be @bitwarden/cli@2026.4.0, and the malicious code was published in 'bw1.js,' a file included in the package contents," the application security company said. "The attack appears to have leveraged

items.title: Trump’s pick for CISA director withdraws from consideration
items.description: Sean Plankey reportedly told colleagues that he needed to focus on assuaging concerns about his Coast Guard work that had led Sen. Rick Scott (R-FL) to block his nomination.

items.title: Microsoft: Some Teams users can’t join meetings after Edge update
items.description: Microsoft confirmed that a recent Microsoft Edge browser update introduced a bug that prevents Windows users from joining Teams meetings. [...]

items.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: $290M DeFi Hack, macOS LotL Abuse, ProxySmart SIM Farms +25 New Stories
items.description: You scroll past one incident and see another that feels familiar, like it should have been fixed years ago, but it still works with small changes. Same bugs. Same mistakes. The supply chain is messy. Packages you did not check are stealing data, adding backdoors, and spreading. Attacking the systems behind apps is easier than breaking the apps themselves. The exploits are simple but still work

items.title: Chinese Cybersecurity Firm’s AI Hacking Claims Draw Comparisons to Claude Mythos
items.description: 360 Digital Security Group claims to have uncovered 1,000 vulnerabilities using AI, including at the Tianfu Cup hacking contest. The post Chinese Cybersecurity Firm’s AI Hacking Claims Draw Comparisons to Claude Mythos appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: UK warns of Chinese hackers using proxy networks to evade detection
items.description: The United Kingdom's National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC-UK) and international partners warned that China-nexus hackers are increasingly using large-scale proxy networks of hijacked consumer devices to evade detection and disguise their malicious activity. [...]

items.title: New GopherWhisper APT group abuses Outlook, Slack, Discord for comms
items.description: A previously undocumented state-backed threat actor named GopherWhisper is using a Go-based custom toolkit and legitimate services like Microsoft 365 Outlook, Slack, and Discord in attacks against government entities. [...]

items.title: [Webinar] Mythos Reality Check: Beating Automated Exploitation at AI Speed
items.description: Imagine a world where hackers don't sleep, don't take breaks, and find weak spots in your systems instantly. Well, that world is already here. Thanks to AI, attackers are now launching automated, large-scale exploits faster than ever before. The time you have to fix a vulnerability before it gets attacked is shrinking to zero. We call this the Collapsing Exploit Window, and it means your

items.title: CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
Rilian Raises $17.5 Million for AI-Native Security Orchestration
items.description: The company will hire new talent and expand operations across the US and other allied countries. The post Rilian Raises $17.5 Million for AI-Native Security Orchestration appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Project Glasswing Proved AI Can Find the Bugs. Who's Going to Fix Them?
items.description: Last week, Anthropic announced Project Glasswing, an AI model so effective at discovering software vulnerabilities that they took the extraordinary step of postponing its public release. Instead, the company has given access to Apple, Microsoft, Google, Amazon, and a coalition of others to find and patch bugs before adversaries can. Mythos Preview, the model that led to Project Glasswing, found

items.title: The Behavioral Shift: Why Trusted Relationships Are the Newest Attack Surface
items.description: New analysis from Abnormal AI reveals how attackers have abandoned technical exploits to weaponize routine workflows and internal trust. The post The Behavioral Shift: Why Trusted Relationships Are the Newest Attack Surface appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA orders feds to patch BlueHammer flaw exploited as zero-day
items.description: CISA has ordered U.S. federal agencies to patch a Microsoft Defender privilege escalation flaw (dubbed BlueHammer) that has been exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]

items.title: Luxury Cosmetics Giant Rituals Discloses Data Breach
items.description: The company is notifying My Rituals members that hackers downloaded part of their data, including names and addresses. The post Luxury Cosmetics Giant Rituals Discloses Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Apple Patches Exploited Notification Flaw, (Thu, Apr 23rd)
items.description: Apple yesterday released iOS/iPadOS 26.4.2 and iOS/iPadOS 18.7.8. This update fixes a single Notification Services vulnerability, CVE-2026-28950:

items.title: AI Can Autonomously Hack Cloud Systems With Minimal Oversight: Researchers
items.description: Palo Alto Networks has developed Zealot, a multi-agent penetration testing PoC capable of reconnaissance, exploitation, and exfiltration. The post AI Can Autonomously Hack Cloud Systems With Minimal Oversight: Researchers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: 'Zealot' Shows What AI's Capable of in Staged Cloud Attack
items.description: The proof of concept revealed AI-based attacks unfold too fast for human defenders to respond, and that AI evinced more autonomous behavior than expected.

items.title: China-Linked GopherWhisper Infects 12 Mongolian Government Systems with Go Backdoors
items.description: Mongolian governmental institutions have emerged as the target of a previously undocumented China-aligned advanced persistent threat (APT) group tracked as GopherWhisper. "The group wields a wide array of tools mostly written in Go, using injectors and loaders to deploy and execute various backdoors in its arsenal," Slovakian cybersecurity company ESET said in a report shared with The Hacker

items.title: Apple Patches iOS Flaw Allowing Recovery of Deleted Chats
items.description: Apple rolled out the security patches for dozens of iPhone and iPad models and generations. The post Apple Patches iOS Flaw Allowing Recovery of Deleted Chats appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Vercel Finds More Compromised Accounts in Context.ai-Linked Breach
items.description: Vercel on Wednesday revealed that it has identified an additional set of customer accounts that were compromised as part of a security incident that enabled unauthorized access to its internal systems. The company said it made the discovery after expanding its investigation to include an extra set of compromise indicators, alongside a review of requests to the Vercel network and environment

items.title: Apple Fixes iOS Flaw That Let FBI Recover Deleted Signal Messages
items.description: Apple has rolled out a software fix for iOS and iPadOS to address a Notification Services flaw that stored notifications marked for deletion on the device. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-28950 (CVSS score: N/A), has been described as a logging issue that has been addressed with improved data redaction. "Notifications marked for deletion could be unexpectedly retained on the device,"

items.title: Recent Microsoft Defender Vulnerability Exploited as Zero-Day
items.description: The flaw allows attackers to access the SAM database, extract NTLM hashes, and gain System privileges. The post Recent Microsoft Defender Vulnerability Exploited as Zero-Day appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Africa Relinquishes Cyberattack Lead to Latin America — For Now
items.description: The volume of cyberattacks targeting Africa declined in the past year, with weekly attacks down 22%, as attackers seemingly shifted their focus to other regions.

items.title: 'The Gentlemen' Rapidly Rises to Ransomware Prominence
items.description: Not nearly as polite as the name suggests, the ransomware gang has impressed researchers with its speed in scaling up operations — and its sophistication.

items.title: Malicious KICS Docker Images and VS Code Extensions Hit Checkmarx Supply Chain
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of malicious images pushed to the official "checkmarx/kics" Docker Hub repository. In an alert published today, software supply chain security company Socket revealed that unknown threat actors managed to have overwritten existing tags, including v2.1.20 and alpine, while also introducing a new v2.1.21 tag that does not correspond to an official release. The

items.title: Self-Propagating Supply Chain Worm Hijacks npm Packages to Steal Developer Tokens
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a fresh set of packages that have been compromised by bad actors to deliver a self-propagating worm that spreads through stolen developer npm tokens. The supply chain worm has been detected by both Socket and StepSecurity, with the companies tracking the activity under the name CanisterSprawl owing to the use of an ICP canister to exfiltrate the stolen data

items.title: DPRK Fake Job Scams Self-Propagate in 'Contagious Interview'
items.description: A compromised developer's repository serves as a worm-like infection vector to spread remote access Trojans (RATs) and other malware.

items.title: After Bluesky, Mastodon Targeted in DDoS Attack
items.description: The DDoS attack caused a major outage, but Mastodon mitigated it within a few hours. The post After Bluesky, Mastodon Targeted in DDoS Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Electricity Is a Growing Area of Cyber Risk
items.description: IT has long been concerned about ensuring systems receive the right amount of electricity. Cyberattackers are realizing they can manipulate voltage fluctuations for their purposes, too.

items.title: Most Serious Cyberattacks Against the UK Now From Russia, Iran and China, Cyber Chief Says
items.description: British businesses need to prepare themselves to defend against cyberattacks because the U.K. could be targeted “at scale,” if it became involved in an international conflict. The post Most Serious Cyberattacks Against the UK Now From Russia, Iran and China, Cyber Chief Says appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.description: &#x5b;This is a Guest Diary by L. Carty, an ISC intern as part of the SANS.edu Bachelor&&#x23&#x3b;39&#x3b;s Degree in Applied Cybersecurity (BACS) program &#x5b;1].]

items.title: Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to BlackCat Scheme
items.description: A cautionary tale illustrates why the person negotiating should never be involved with any part of the ransom payment process.

items.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: A .WAV With A Payload, (Tue, Apr 21st)
items.description: There have been reports of threat actors using a .wav file as a vector for malware.

items.title: Handling the CVE Flood With EPSS, (Mon, Apr 20th)
items.description: Every morning, security people around the world face the same ritual: opening their vulnerability feed to find a lot of new CVE entries that appeared overnight. Over the past decade, this flood has become a defining challenge of modern defensive security. Some numbers[1]:

items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from organizations using Windows. Here are a few highlights from this month's Patch Tuesday.

items.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
items.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and novice code jockey.

items.title: Who is the Kimwolf Botmaster “Dort”?
items.description: In early January 2026, KrebsOnSecurity revealed how a security researcher disclosed a vulnerability that was used to assemble Kimwolf, the world's largest and most disruptive botnet. Since then, the person in control of Kimwolf -- who goes by the handle "Dort" -- has coordinated a barrage of distributed denial-of-service (DDoS), doxing and email flooding attacks against the researcher and this author, and more recently caused a SWAT team to be sent to the researcher's home. This post examines what is knowable about Dort based on public information.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
CISA Orders Federal Agencies to Strengthen Edge Device Security Amid Rising Cyber Threats
CISA Urges Critical Infrastructure Organizations to Take Action Against Insider Threats
CISA Releases Product Categories List to Propel Post-Quantum Cryptography Adoption Pursuant to President Trump’s Executive Order 14306
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
